chapter_title.ar: باب الْبَعِيرِ وَ الثَّوْرِ يَمْتَنِعَانِ مِنَ الذَّبْحِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 5 – The camel and the bull both preventing from being slaughtered

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/11)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/11)
text: إِنَّ ثَوْراً بِالْكُوفَةِ ثَارَ فَبَادَرَ النَّاسُ إِلَيْهِ بِأَسْيَافِهِمْ فَضَرَبُوهُ فَأَتَوْا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فَسَأَلُوهُ فَقَالَ ذَكَاةٌ وَحِيَّةٌ وَ لَحْمُهُ حَلَالٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Safwan, from Ays Bin Al Qasim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘A bull at Al-Kufa went on rampage, so the people followed it with their swords, so they struck it down. So they came to Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>, and they asked him<sup>asws</sup>. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘A vivid slaughter, and its flesh is Permissible’.
en.sarwar: 2. Abu Ali al-Ash‘ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar and Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from ibn Muskan from Muhammad al-Halabiy who has said the following: “About the case of a bull that broke loose and people rushed with their swords and beat it down, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘They asked ’Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about it and he (the Imam) said, ‘It is a fast slaughtering but its flesh is lawful for food.’”
